briefs.title: Burnaby to Appoint Chief Election Officer for 2026 Election
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby plans to appoint a Chief Election Officer to manage the 2026 General Local Election. This officer will be in charge of running the election according to local election laws.
briefs.source_body:
7.1
APPOINTMENT OF CHIEF ELECTION OFFICER
29

Purpose: To recommend the appointment of the Chief Election Officer for
the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road BIA Renewal Approved for 5 Years
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved the renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) for another five years. A BIA helps local businesses work together to improve their commercial area. The city received enough support from businesses in the area to move forward with the renewal.
briefs.source_body:
7.2
CERTIFICATE OF SUFFICIENCY: 2026 BURNABY NORTH ROAD BIA
RENEWAL
31
Purpose: To provide results for the petitions submitted against the
proposed North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) renewal bylaw
for a 5-year period.
see Bylaw #14797

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority: Shareholder Approval Needed for 2026
briefs.summary: The Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA) needs Council approval for annual shareholder corporate maintenance matters for 2026. This is a standard yearly requirement to keep the corporation in good standing.
briefs.action_required: No action is required. This is for awareness.
briefs.source_body:
7.3
BHA - SHAREHOLDER'S CORPORATE MAINTENANCE MATTERS
FOR 2026
37
Purpose: To obtain Council approval with respect to certain annual
shareholder’s corporate maintenance matters for 2026 on behalf of the
Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA).

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority Presents 2025 Financials, 2026 Budget
briefs.summary: The Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) presented its financial statements for 2025 and its operating budget for 2026. This shows how the BHA is managing money and planning for the future.
briefs.source_body:
7.4
BHA - 2025 FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND 2026 OPERATING
BUDGET
41
Purpose: To present the Burnaby Housing Authority’s (BHA) 2025
Annual Financial Statements and 2026 Annual Operating Budget.

briefs.title: Burnaby Considers E-Bike, Scooter Bylaw Changes
briefs.summary: Burnaby is thinking about changing its bylaws to deal with electric bikes and scooters. The changes would support a pilot program for shared e-bikes and address the province's electric kick scooter pilot program within the city.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about the bylaw changes if you operate e-bikes or scooters in Burnaby.
briefs.source_body:
7.5
PROPOSED BYLAW AMENDMENTS TO ADDRESS E-BIKE SHARE
PILOT PROGRAM AND PROVINCIAL ELECTRIC KICK SCOOTER
PILOT PROGRAM
65
Purpose: To propose amendments to the Burnaby Street and Traffic
Bylaw and the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw to implement the E-Bike
Share Pilot Program and address the provincial Electric Kick Scooter
Pilot Program within the City.

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Target: City Must Report Progress to BC Government
briefs.summary: Burnaby must send a report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs. This report shows how the city is doing on meeting the housing targets set by the province. This is required by the Housing Supply Act (a law to increase housing) and its rules.
briefs.action_required: No action is required. This is for awareness.
briefs.source_body:
7.6
PROVINCIAL HOUSING TARGET ORDER 6-MONTH INTERIM
PROGRESS REPORT
72
Purpose: To present the Housing Target Order and obtain Council
authorization to forward Burnaby’s Housing Target Progress Report Form
to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs, as required by the
Housing Supply Act and Housing Supply Regulation.

briefs.title: Building Benchmark BC program seeking participants for Year 7
briefs.summary: Building Benchmark BC wants to measure energy use in new buildings. The program helps make buildings more energy-efficient. They are asking for authorization to participate in Year 7 of the program.
briefs.action_required: Consider participating in Building Benchmark BC to improve energy efficiency.
briefs.source_body:
7.7
BUILDING BENCHMARK BC
90
Purpose: To update Council on Building Benchmark BC and seek
authorization to participate in Year 7.

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves FortisBC Right-of-Way on Pandora St & Gaglardi Way
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved a statutory right-of-way (legal permission) for FortisBC. This allows FortisBC to install, operate, and maintain natural gas equipment on parts of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way.
briefs.source_body:
7.8
7950 PANDORA ST AND 1200 GAGLARDI WAY - BURNABY
114
2
MOUNTAIN - STATUTORY RIGHT-OF-WAY FOR FORTIS BC
Purpose: To seek Council approval to grant Statutory Right-of-Way in
favour of FortisBC Energy Inc. (FortisBC) over a portion of 7950 Pandora
Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way to permit the installation, operations and
maintenance of natural gas works.

briefs.title: Modular Housing Four-Plex Development Contract Approval Sought
briefs.summary: A contract award needs council approval for a pilot project. The project involves developing a modular four-plex housing unit. This aims to increase housing options.
briefs.source_body:
7.9
CA - DEVELOPMENT OF MODULAR HOUSING FOUR-PLEX AT 8315
14TH AVENUE
120
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the
development of a modular four-plex housing unit as a pilot project.

briefs.title: Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Contract Approval Sought
briefs.summary: The Council is seeking approval for a contract to replace the Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert. This ensures necessary infrastructure upgrades are completed.
briefs.source_body:
7.10
CA - (CB-8039) JERRY ROGERS CREEK CULVERT REPLACEMENT
123
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the
Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Project.

briefs.title: Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement Contract Approved
briefs.summary: The city has approved a contract for replacing a culvert (a tunnel carrying a stream or drain under a road) and paving work on the Central Valley Greenway. This ensures the continued function and safety of the greenway.
briefs.source_body:
7.11
CA - (CB-3264) CENTRAL VALLEY GREENWAY CULVERT
REPLACEMENT AND PAVING WORKS
125
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the
Central Valley Greenway Culvert Replacement and Paving Project.

briefs.title: Cellular Service Contract Change Approved
briefs.summary: The city council approved a change to the contract for cellular voice and data services. This means the city has agreed to new terms or conditions with its cell service provider.
briefs.source_body:
7.12
CC - TELEPHONY - CELLULAR VOICE AND DATA SERVICES
127
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for
Telephony - Cellular Voice and Data Services.

briefs.title: City Seeks Approval for HVAC Maintenance Contract Change
briefs.summary: The City is asking for approval to change its contract for heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) system maintenance and repair. This change affects services at City-owned buildings. The details of the specific changes were not provided in the article.
briefs.locations: British Columbia, Vancouver, Burnaby, Richmond, Surrey, New Westminster, Coquitlam, Delta, Langley, Abbotsford, Kelowna, Victoria
briefs.source_body:
7.13
CC - HVAC SYSTEM MAINTENANCE & REPAIR SERVICES
134
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a contract change (CC) for
HVAC system maintenance & repair services at various City-owned
buildings.

briefs.title: Cisco WebEx Contact Centre Licence Renewal Approved: Council Decision
briefs.summary: The council has approved renewing the contract for the Cisco WebEx Contact Centre Licence Subscription. This licence lets the council use Cisco WebEx for its contact centre.
briefs.source_body:
7.14
CR - CISCO WEBEX CONTACT CENTER LICENSE SUBSCRIPTION
142
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for
Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Subscription.

briefs.title: Fire Protection Contract Renewal Approved
briefs.summary: The city council approved renewing a contract for fire protection systems and equipment installation, maintenance, and repair services. This ensures these services will continue to be available.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Hotel & Accommodation
briefs.source_body:
7.15
CR - FIRE PROTECTION SYSTEMS AND EQUIPMENT
INSTALLATION MAINTENANCE AND REPAIR SERVICES
146
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for
Fire Protection Systems and Equipment Installation Maintenance and
Repair Services.

briefs.title: City of Burnaby to Review Financial Practices
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby will hire an external reviewer. This review will help protect the City's money and financial health. It also aims to lower the risk of higher taxes and service costs for businesses and residents.
briefs.action_required: No action is required. Stay informed about potential changes.
briefs.source_body:
8.1
FMC - EXTERNAL REVIEW OF CITY FINANCIAL PRACTICES
153
Purpose: To seek Council approval to engage an external reviewer to
help safeguard the City's assets and financial standing, and to mitigate
the risk of significant tax increases and rising service costs for
businesses and residents.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council to consider 20-storey apartment development near Royal Oak
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider forwarding a proposal for a 20-storey apartment building at 5035 Beresford Street to a future Council meeting for First and Second Reading. This is part of Rezoning application #25-11 and the Royal Oak Community Plan.
briefs.action_required: Monitor City Council meetings for updates and opportunities to provide feedback.
briefs.source_body:
8.2
PDC - REZ #25-11 - 5035 BERESFORD STREET - PROPOSED 20-
STOREY APARTMENT DEVELOPMENT (ROYAL OAK COMMUNITY
PLAN)
154
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #25-11 to First
and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.

briefs.title: City of Burnaby 2026 Investment Forecast Provided in Year-End Report
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby has released an update on its 2025 Investment Program. The report also includes a forecast for 2026. This information helps the City plan for future projects and manage its finances.
briefs.source_body:
9.2
FMC - CITY INVESTMENTS - 2025 YEAR END REPORT
177
Purpose: To provide Council with an update on the 2025 Investment Program and present a forecast
for 2026.

briefs.title: Burnaby Civic Projects: March 2026 Status Update
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council received an update on significant civic projects in March 2026. The update covered progress on various construction and development projects in the city.
briefs.source_body:
9.3
FMC - SU: SIGNIFICANT CIVIC PROJECTS - MARCH 2026
177
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on significant civic projects.

briefs.title: Burnaby North Road Businesses: New Business Improvement Area Bylaw Rejected
briefs.summary: A proposed bylaw to create a Business Improvement Area (BIA) for Burnaby North Road has been rejected. A BIA lets local businesses work together to improve their area. The bylaw won't proceed because enough businesses opposed it.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Professional Services, Healthcare, Finance & Insurance, Technology, Natural Resources, Transportation & Logistics
briefs.source_body:
11.1
 FINAL ADOPTION
11.1.1
#14797 - Burnaby Business Improvement Area (Burnaby North
Road) Bylaw 2026
182
Purpose: to designate the area of the Burnaby North Road
Business Improvement Association
(Item 7.3., Administrative Report, Council February 24, 2026)
see Item 7.2.
NOTE: sufficient petitions against the proposed BIA have been
received

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves Changes to Parking and EV Charging Bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby has officially adopted Amendment Bylaw #14805. This updates the rules for parking meters and electric vehicle (EV) charging meters in the city. The bylaw likely includes changes to parking fees, hours, or EV charging regulations.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated bylaw to understand any changes to parking or EV charging rules.
briefs.source_body:
11.2
 FINAL ADOPTION
11.2.1 #14805 - Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter
Bylaw 2019, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026

briefs.title: Burnaby Fees & Charges Bylaw Updated
briefs.summary: Burnaby updated its Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This bylaw lists all the fees and charges for various city services and permits. The changes are in Bylaw No. 2, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Check the updated bylaw to see if any fees relevant to your business have changed.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.2 #14806 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw, Amendment
Bylaw No. 2, 2026

briefs.title: Burnaby Updates Bylaw Enforcement for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby has updated its Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. This bylaw lets the city fine people for breaking certain rules. The update is called Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026.
briefs.source_body:
11.2.3 #14807 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment
Bylaw No. 2, 2026
